posts.text:
Lady, your sloppy resolution would have doxxed victim statements that had identifying info.

It redacted names & NOTHING else. House empl. records can match statements to reverse ID names.

Take your job seriously. Fix your messy loopholes & I’d vote YES. You know that. Not hard!


Nancy Mace (@NancyMace)

AOC voted against releasing sexual harassment records of Members of Congress. But wants to lecture you on Epstein...

posts.text: [VIDEO: How we beat authoritarianism. | Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez] Thank you. It's funny that you asked this question about Gito because in many ways I relate to him. I share many aspects of his story. I was first elected um when I was uh in in 2018 and I was a waitress in New York City. I had no health insurance. I could not pay my rent. And I consistently saw a party that championed special interests, the elite, um, and a Democratic party that was very much kind of telling working people that everything was fine when it in fact wasn't. And after I was elected, I remember I was crossing this threshold and there's this closed-d dooror meeting of many high-ranking Democratic Party officials, people that you see in the news and all of that. And they uh they derided behind closed doors at that time, a long time ago, they derided many of the populist demands

Trump ran on a campaign promise to the American people that ICE would go after the “worst of the worst.”

The reality is that of the more than 73,000+ people in ICE detention right now, 92% of those people have ZERO criminal record. This administration is using ICE as much more of an anti-civilian force meant for political persecution of this president’s perceived enemies.
